pageMeta.title: AI Agents for Faster Towing Dispatch and Support
pageMeta.description: DocsBot helps towing companies answer callers, collect roadside details, route urgent jobs, and keep operators aligned from approved SOPs.

hero.eyebrow: Roadside support automation for towing and recovery teams
hero.headline: DocsBot AI agents keep towing calls moving when every minute matters
hero.subheadline: Train a DocsBot agent on your service policies, pricing guidelines, coverage areas, impound instructions, safety procedures, and customer FAQs so stranded drivers get fast answers while dispatchers receive cleaner handoffs.

proofBullets.text: Collect location, vehicle, incident, destination, and contact details before a dispatcher steps in.
Answer service-area, impound, storage, payment, equipment, and roadside-assistance questions from approved sources.
Route urgent requests, create summaries, trigger configured workflows, and escalate sensitive situations to humans.
Give office staff and drivers a private knowledge agent for SOPs, safety checklists, and policy lookups.

businessClarity.whatDocsBotDoes: DocsBot helps towing companies create AI agents trained on their own documents, website, FAQs, SOPs, forms, service rules, and internal knowledge. Agents can answer source-grounded questions, collect structured intake details, summarize conversations, surface knowledge gaps, and take scoped actions through customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, MCP Connectors, or workflow automations.

whoItHelps.roleContext: High call volume, incomplete roadside details, and repeat status questions can slow down dispatch. DocsBot helps standardize intake and handoffs without replacing human judgment on safety-critical or complex jobs.

bullets.text: Capture key job details such as vehicle type, exact location, roadside position, destination, membership status, and special equipment needs.
Give customers clear next steps for routine lockout, jump-start, tire-change, winch-out, and tow requests from your approved policies.
Prepare concise handoff summaries for dispatchers, helpdesk tickets, email, forms, or other customer-configured workflows.

whoItHelps.roleContext: The U.S. automobile towing industry is estimated at $14.5 billion in 2025 with more than 40,000 businesses, making speed, trust, and local responsiveness important differentiators.

bullets.text: Use analytics and conversation review to see which questions repeat, which policies are unclear, and where customers get stuck.
Support 24/7 self-service for pricing explanations, release requirements, accepted payments, office hours, and service boundaries.
Qualify commercial, fleet, municipal, apartment, and repair-shop leads with structured intake before sending summaries to sales or operations.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Tow operators work around traffic, disabled vehicles, equipment, weather, and customer stress. DocsBot can make approved procedures easier to find, while people remain responsible for现场 safety decisions.

bullets.text: Search private SOPs, equipment guides, recovery procedures, incident forms, and customer-service scripts from a phone or team chat experience.
Pull up safety reminders and escalation steps before complex recovery, impound, heavy-duty, or roadside incidents.
Summarize incident notes for office follow-up, insurance documentation, billing review, or manager handoff when configured.

whoItHelps.roleContext: After the tow, customers often ask about invoices, vehicle release, storage fees, documents, receipts, and damage claims. A source-grounded agent helps keep responses consistent.

bullets.text: Answer release-document, storage, lien, office-hour, payment, and receipt questions from approved public and internal sources.
Draft ticket or email responses for staff review when connected to helpdesk-style workflows.
Escalate complaints, legal questions, damage claims, or disputed charges to the right human team with conversation context.

evidenceSources.metric: $14.5 billion U.S. automobile towing revenue in 2025
evidenceSources.claim: IBISWorld estimates the U.S. automobile towing industry at $14.5 billion in 2025, with 102,000 employees and 40,287 businesses.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Automobile Towing in the US - Market Research Report (2015-2030)

evidenceSources.claim: The U.S. Census NAICS definition covers establishments primarily engaged in towing light or heavy motor vehicles, both local and long distance.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: 1997 NAICS Manual, Sector 48: 488410 Motor Vehicle Towing

evidenceSources.metric: 231 work-related deaths in motor vehicle towing from 2011 to 2017
evidenceSources.claim: A Journal of Safety Research article indexed by CDC reports 231 work-related deaths in motor vehicle towing from 2011 to 2017; 37% involved operators struck by roadway traffic.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Motor vehicle towing: An analysis of injuries in a high-risk yet understudied industry

evidenceSources.metric: More than 21,000 U.S. workers died in work-related motor vehicle crashes from 2011 to 2022
evidenceSources.claim: CDC NIOSH says more than 21,000 U.S. workers died in work-related motor vehicle crashes from 2011 to 2022, representing 35% of all work-related deaths.

agentUseCases.name: Roadside request intake and dispatch handoff
agentUseCases.problem: Callers may be stressed, unsure of their exact location, or missing details dispatch needs before assigning the right truck.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: A customer-facing DocsBot agent asks structured questions, confirms vehicle and location details, explains immediate next steps from your approved policies, and prepares a dispatch-ready summary for a human or configured workflow.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, or forms, the agent can submit an intake record, notify dispatch, create a task, or attach the conversation summary to a ticketing workflow.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: I’m on the shoulder with a flat tire. What information do you need?
Can you tow an all-wheel-drive SUV from a parking garage?
My car is blocking traffic. How do I request urgent help?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Fewer incomplete calls, cleaner dispatch handoffs, and a more consistent experience for stranded drivers.

agentUseCases.name: Impound and vehicle release guidance
agentUseCases.problem: Impound customers often ask the same document, payment, fee, and office-hour questions, while sensitive disputes still need staff review.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers from your impound policies, release requirements, storage-fee explanations, accepted payment rules, and public pages. It can collect missing details and escalate disputes, legal questions, or complaints to staff with a summary.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: When approved, AI Actions can look up a record status, create a callback request, route a complaint, or send a release-document checklist through configured systems.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What do I need to bring to release my vehicle?
Can someone else pick up my car for me?
Why was I charged a storage fee?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Reduced repetitive phone calls and clearer, more consistent answers for vehicle owners.

agentUseCases.name: Fleet, repair-shop, and property-account qualification
agentUseCases.problem: Commercial accounts can be valuable, but owners and managers need to separate urgent service requests from partnership, contract, and pricing inquiries.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: A DocsBot presales agent answers service-fit questions from approved pages and collects company name, location, vehicle types, expected volume, service needs, and preferred follow-up. It applies your qualification rules before forwarding a summary.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Configured workflows can send qualified leads to CRM-style systems, email, helpdesk software, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or a scheduling flow for a human follow-up.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Do you offer towing for apartment complexes?
Can you support a delivery fleet with after-hours breakdowns?
We run a repair shop. How do we set up a preferred towing account?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: More complete lead details and faster follow-up for higher-value commercial relationships.

agentUseCases.name: Driver SOP and roadside safety knowledge agent
agentUseCases.problem: Tow operators need quick access to procedures in a high-risk work environment; research reports 231 work-related deaths in motor vehicle towing from 2011 to 2017, with 37% involving operators struck by roadway traffic.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: A private DocsBot agent answers from SOPs, equipment manuals, training files, incident procedures, and safety checklists. Drivers and supervisors can ask for the right procedure, documentation step, or escalation path before or after a job.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: The agent can summarize an incident note, create a supervisor review task, or route a safety concern through approved internal workflows while leaving现场 judgment to trained personnel.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What is our cone-placement checklist for freeway work?
What photos do we need after a recovery job?
Who reviews a damage claim after a tow?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Faster access to approved procedures and better documentation consistency across shifts.

agentUseCases.name: After-hours customer self-service
agentUseCases.problem: Towing companies get questions outside office hours about rates, coverage, release windows, receipts, and service limitations.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers from your website, FAQs, policy documents, and service catalog in the company voice. If the request needs a person, it collects context and creates a handoff for the next available team member.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: A website widget can support public self-service, while approved actions can create tickets, trigger notifications, or request a callback.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Do you take credit cards at the lot?
Can I get a receipt for an insurance claim?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Always-available answers for routine questions without forcing staff to repeat the same information.

agentUseCases.name: Knowledge-gap reporting for recurring towing questions
agentUseCases.problem: If customers repeatedly ask about the same fees, coverage limits, or release steps, the website and scripts may not be clear enough.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot analytics, logs, reports, topic management, and conversation review help teams see unresolved questions and repeated topics so managers can improve FAQs, policies, dispatcher scripts, and training content.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Managers can review conversations, identify content gaps, and update approved sources so the agent improves future answers from better documentation.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Which questions did customers ask most after business hours?
Where did the agent lack source material?
What topics should we add to our impound FAQ?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Better documentation, fewer repeat calls, and clearer customer communication over time.

customerFacingAgent.headline: Give stranded drivers clear answers before the dispatcher joins
customerFacingAgent.intro: A DocsBot customer-facing agent can live on your website or support experience, answer from approved towing content, collect structured details, and escalate to people when a job is urgent, sensitive, or requires operational judgment.

requests.request: “I need a tow now. What details do you need?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Ask for location, vehicle, roadside position, destination, keys, contact information, safety conditions, and special equipment needs, then prepare a dispatch-ready summary.
requests.optionalAction: Create a request record or notify dispatch through a configured Skill, API, connector, form, or workflow.

requests.request: “Can you tow my motorcycle, EV, AWD car, or heavy truck?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Answer from approved service catalogs and equipment guidance, explain when a human confirmation is needed, and collect vehicle details for follow-up.
requests.optionalAction: Route equipment-sensitive requests to the correct dispatcher or supervisor with a concise summary.

requests.request: “What do I need to retrieve my impounded vehicle?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Provide release steps, office hours, payment options, required documents, and escalation paths from approved impound policies.
requests.optionalAction: Collect contact details for a callback or create a review task for disputed, legal, or damage-related questions.

requests.request: “Can I get an estimate or set up a fleet account?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Collect route, vehicle, service type, account details, expected volume, and timeline while keeping pricing commitments or contracts with staff.
requests.optionalAction: Forward qualified lead details to sales, email, CRM-style workflows, or scheduling tools when configured.

requests.request: “Do you speak Spanish or another language?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: DocsBot agents can answer in more than 100 languages using your approved source material, helping multilingual customers understand next steps.
requests.optionalAction: Escalate to a bilingual staff member or callback queue when a conversation requires human support.

requests.agentResponseStrategy: Explain standard receipt, invoice, and documentation processes from approved policies and ask for the details staff need to locate the record.
requests.optionalAction: Create a ticket or route a billing request through an approved helpdesk-style workflow.

internalAgent.headline: Put towing procedures, policies, and job notes within reach
internalAgent.intro: DocsBot can also work as a private internal knowledge agent for office staff, dispatchers, supervisors, drivers, and managers. Train it on SOPs, PDFs, manuals, wikis, spreadsheets, policies, and training materials so teams can ask questions instead of hunting through folders.

workflows.name: Dispatcher procedure lookup
workflows.problem: Dispatchers need consistent answers for coverage, equipment matching, after-hours rules, police rotation work, and escalation steps.
workflows.agentWorkflow: Staff ask the internal agent for the relevant procedure, script, or checklist. DocsBot returns source-grounded answers and links back to approved documentation.
workflows.optionalAction: Draft a ticket response, summarize a call, or create a follow-up task through a configured workflow.
workflows.outcome: More consistent dispatch guidance across shifts and less time searching for procedures.

workflows.name: Driver onboarding and training support
workflows.problem: New drivers need fast access to training material, safety steps, paperwork requirements, and customer-service expectations.
workflows.agentWorkflow: DocsBot answers onboarding questions from manuals, training PDFs, videos summaries, SOPs, and policy documents, then highlights topics that still need manager instruction.
workflows.optionalAction: Generate a summary of common trainee questions for supervisors to review.
workflows.outcome: Faster onboarding support and clearer visibility into training gaps.

workflows.name: Incident and claim documentation support
workflows.problem: Towing incidents can require photos, notes, approvals, customer communication, and insurance or legal follow-up.
workflows.agentWorkflow: The agent retrieves the correct documentation checklist, drafts an internal summary, and routes sensitive claim or damage questions to authorized staff.
workflows.optionalAction: Create a review task, update approved fields, or notify a manager when configured with scoped permissions.
workflows.outcome: More complete documentation and cleaner handoffs for situations that need review.

workflows.name: Owner reporting and content improvement
workflows.problem: Owners may not know which questions are consuming staff time or where documentation is missing.
workflows.agentWorkflow: DocsBot reports, analytics, logs, topic management, and conversation review reveal repeated customer and employee questions, unanswered topics, and opportunities to improve content.
workflows.optionalAction: Export insights or create documentation tasks through approved workflow automations.
workflows.outcome: A practical feedback loop for better FAQs, scripts, and internal knowledge.

docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Train an agent on service areas, towing policies, impound rules, payment guidance, equipment capabilities, release requirements, and dispatcher scripts.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot delivers instant answers in the company’s voice from approved knowledge sources and supports human escalation when needed.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Give stranded drivers and vehicle owners consistent answers about towing requests, after-hours support, impound release, receipts, and service limitations.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot AI Actions and Skills can perform bounded live lookups, record creation, updates, workflow triggers, routing, summaries, and handoffs through configured access.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Collect roadside intake, create a dispatch task, submit a callback request, route a billing dispute, or notify a supervisor through customer-approved workflows.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot can act as a private assistant for employees, with access controls, role-based permissions, and training on internal documents and policies.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Let dispatchers, office staff, and supervisors search SOPs, safety procedures, equipment guides, onboarding files, and incident documentation rules.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot includes analytics, logs, topic management, reports, conversation review, and knowledge-gap discovery.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Find recurring questions about fees, release documents, coverage areas, wait expectations, commercial accounts, and policies that need clearer public content.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot offers SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR-ready governance, encryption in transit and at rest, permissions, and controls over what agents can access and do.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Protect internal SOPs, customer conversation history, and operational workflows while defining boundaries for sensitive claims, disputes, billing, and legal questions.

trustClaims: Proven at high support volume, including Sony NURO’s 30,000 inquiries in one month with about 80% resolution.
Train agents on your own manuals, website, policies, and support content so answers stay grounded in approved sources.
Deploy customer-facing and staff-assist experiences that help teams save time on repetitive first-line questions.
trustAndProof.whyTheseReviewsFit: Towing teams need fast first-line answers, searchable operating knowledge, and support workflows that still hand sensitive situations to people. DocsBot proof spans high-volume customer support, 24/7 documentation-based assistance, website deployment, and human support team assistance.

schemaHints.serviceType: AI support agents and internal knowledge automation for towing companies

webSearchQueries: BLS motor vehicle towing industry employment NAICS 488410 establishments revenue
IBISWorld automobile towing industry US revenue 2024
OSHA tow truck driver struck by roadside hazards towing
CDC NIOSH motor vehicle towing injuries 231 work related deaths 2011 2016

faq.question: Can an AI agent collect enough roadside details before a dispatcher takes over?
faq.answer: Yes. A DocsBot agent can ask for the caller’s location, roadside position, vehicle type, destination, keys, contact details, safety conditions, membership status, and special equipment needs. The goal is not to replace dispatch judgment; it is to give dispatchers a cleaner summary so they spend less time re-asking basics.

faq.question: How would this handle urgent calls where a vehicle is blocking traffic or the driver is unsafe?
faq.answer: DocsBot can be configured to recognize urgent or sensitive situations, collect the minimum useful context, and escalate to a human dispatcher or configured workflow. For safety-critical roadside decisions, the agent should provide approved instructions and hand off quickly rather than trying to decide truck assignment or scene response on its own.

faq.question: Will the agent make pricing promises that create disputes later?
faq.answer: It should not be set up that way. You can train DocsBot on approved rate explanations, fee policies, service boundaries, and estimate language, then instruct it to avoid firm commitments when a dispatcher or manager must confirm distance, equipment, police rotation rules, after-hours fees, storage, or unusual recovery work.

faq.question: Can DocsBot answer impound release questions without giving legal advice?
faq.answer: Yes. It can answer from your approved impound policies, release checklists, payment rules, office hours, and document requirements. Disputes, damage claims, lien issues, law-enforcement questions, or legal interpretations can be routed to staff with the conversation summary so the agent stays within defined boundaries.

faq.question: What information do we need to prepare before launching a towing AI agent?
faq.answer: Most towing teams start with service-area pages, dispatcher scripts, intake forms, rate guidance, impound release rules, payment policies, FAQs, fleet-account criteria, SOPs, safety checklists, and escalation rules. The cleaner those sources are, the more consistently DocsBot can answer and hand off work.

faq.question: Can the agent work with our dispatch, helpdesk, CRM, or forms system?
faq.answer: DocsBot can support integrations through customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, MCP Connectors, forms, webhooks, or workflow automations. Typical actions include creating a request record, sending a summary to dispatch, routing a billing issue, notifying a team, or forwarding a commercial lead. Specific system access depends on what you authorize and configure.

faq.question: Can we use this for after-hours calls without making customers feel abandoned?
faq.answer: Yes. A website or support agent can answer routine after-hours questions about coverage, accepted payments, office hours, receipts, release requirements, and service limitations. For service requests or sensitive issues, it can collect context and create a callback, ticket, or dispatch handoff through approved workflows.

faq.question: How do we keep the agent from giving different answers than our dispatchers?
faq.answer: Train it on the same approved scripts, policies, service matrices, and SOPs your team uses. DocsBot provides source-grounded answers and links back to approved material, while analytics and conversation review help managers spot unclear policies, missing content, or recurring questions that need better documentation.

faq.question: Can drivers use DocsBot internally for SOPs and safety checklists?
faq.answer: Yes. A private internal DocsBot agent can be trained on driver manuals, equipment guides, recovery procedures, incident forms, safety checklists, and escalation steps. It can help drivers and supervisors find approved procedures faster, while leaving现场 safety decisions to trained personnel.

faq.question: Why is human escalation so important for towing workflows?
faq.answer: Towing involves real roadside risk. A CDC-indexed analysis reported 231 work-related deaths in motor vehicle towing from 2011 to 2017, and 37% involved operators struck by roadway traffic. DocsBot is best used to retrieve procedures, collect details, and route context—not to replace trained judgment in hazardous situations.

faq.question: Can the agent support heavy-duty, motorcycle, EV, AWD, or parking-garage towing questions?
faq.answer: Yes, if your source material covers those services. DocsBot can answer from your service catalog, equipment guidance, and vehicle-handling policies, then escalate when a dispatcher needs to confirm clearance, weight, drivetrain handling, battery concerns, or specialized equipment availability.

faq.question: How would this help with fleet, repair-shop, property-manager, or municipal account leads?
faq.answer: A DocsBot presales agent can collect company name, locations, vehicle types, expected volume, service needs, contract interest, urgency, and preferred follow-up. It can apply your qualification rules and forward a structured summary to sales, operations, email, CRM-style workflows, or scheduling tools when configured.

faq.question: What ROI should a towing company look for beyond call deflection?
faq.answer: Look at cleaner dispatch handoffs, fewer incomplete intake records, reduced repeat questions about impound and billing, faster after-hours response capture, better commercial lead qualification, and less time searching for SOPs. For a fragmented U.S. towing market estimated at $14.5 billion in 2025 with more than 40,000 businesses, small service improvements can matter competitively.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help us see which customer questions are driving the most calls?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot includes analytics, logs, reports, topic management, conversation review, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery. Owners and managers can use this to identify repeated questions about fees, release documents, coverage areas, hours, wait expectations, and policies that need clearer public content.

faq.question: How do we protect customer details, internal SOPs, and operational information?
faq.answer: DocsBot offers SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR-ready governance, encryption in transit and at rest, granular access controls, and role-based permissions. It also does not use customer data to train public models. For actions and integrations, customers define what the agent can access, log, and do.

faq.question: Can we separate what customers see from what dispatchers and drivers can ask internally?
faq.answer: Yes. You can use customer-facing agents for public service, impound, billing, and intake questions while using private internal agents for SOPs, safety procedures, driver onboarding, equipment guides, and claims documentation. Access controls and role-based permissions help keep internal content out of public experiences.

faq.question: Will this work for a small towing company that does not have a formal knowledge base?
faq.answer: Yes, but you will still need source material. Many teams start with website pages, PDFs, Word documents, spreadsheets, rate sheets, release checklists, dispatcher scripts, and saved email templates. DocsBot can train on those approved sources, and conversation review can show which missing policies should be documented next.

faq.question: How much training will dispatchers and office staff need?
faq.answer: Training is usually about workflow and boundaries: when to trust a source-grounded answer, how to review summaries, when to escalate, and how to flag missing or outdated content. Dispatchers do not need to become AI engineers, but managers should assign ownership for policy updates and conversation review.

faq.question: Can DocsBot answer in Spanish or other languages for stranded drivers?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot agents can answer in more than 100 languages using your approved source material. For towing, that can help multilingual customers understand service steps, release requirements, payment policies, and callback expectations, while still escalating to bilingual staff when the situation requires human support.

faq.question: How do we handle damage claims, angry callers, or disputed storage fees?
faq.answer: Configure the agent to provide approved process information only, then escalate disputes, threats, legal questions, damage claims, or charge disagreements to the right staff member. DocsBot can collect the vehicle, invoice, tow date, photos or documentation notes, and complaint summary so the human follow-up starts with context.

faq.question: Can the agent help with receipts, invoices, and insurance paperwork requests?
faq.answer: Yes. If trained on your billing policies and documentation procedures, DocsBot can explain how customers request receipts, what details staff need, and expected next steps. With approved workflow access, it can create a ticket, route the request, or draft a response for staff review.

faq.question: How does DocsBot compare with adding another phone menu or basic website chatbot?
faq.answer: A phone menu routes callers, and a basic chatbot often follows fixed scripts. DocsBot is designed as a source-grounded AI agent trained on your towing knowledge, with analytics, human escalation, internal knowledge use, and optional scoped actions such as summaries, routing, ticket creation, notifications, or workflow triggers.

faq.question: Can this scale across multiple lots, shifts, service areas, or franchise-style operations?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can be trained on location-specific pages, coverage rules, office hours, release requirements, payment policies, and internal SOPs. The key implementation decision is content governance: who owns updates when a lot changes hours, a service area changes, or a policy differs by location.

faq.question: Who should own the project internally: dispatch, operations, IT, or customer service?
faq.answer: Most successful evaluations involve all four perspectives. Operations defines policy and risk boundaries, dispatch validates intake and escalation, customer service reviews public-facing answers, and IT or an admin supports data access and workflow connections. An owner or operations leader should decide final scope and success metrics.

faq.question: What metrics should we review after the first month?
faq.answer: Track the number of conversations, common topics, resolved versus escalated questions, incomplete intake rate, after-hours requests captured, commercial leads collected, billing or impound questions reduced, and staff feedback on handoff quality. DocsBot’s analytics and conversation review help turn those findings into better FAQs, scripts, and SOPs.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help standardize onboarding for new drivers and dispatchers?
faq.answer: Yes. A private internal agent can answer from onboarding documents, SOPs, safety checklists, equipment guides, HR policies, and customer-service scripts. Supervisors can also review common trainee questions to see where training materials are unclear or where manager-led instruction is still needed.

faq.question: What should procurement or ownership ask before approving an AI agent for towing operations?
faq.answer: Ask which workflows are in scope, which content sources are approved, what actions the agent can take, how human escalation works, who owns updates, what data is stored, what access controls apply, and which KPIs define success. For towing, boundaries around safety, pricing, impound disputes, and claims should be explicit.
